title: Fix inouts on deep and/or statements consuming all memory
body:
When running `verilator --cc` with [this](https://github.com/lac-dcc/chimera/blob/main/chigen_programs/10k_programs_per_k/4gram/4_gram_verilog_file_2215.v) file, Verilator terminates with the following error:
```
%Error: Verilator threw signal 9. Suggest trying --debug --gdbbt
```
When adding the --debug and --gdbbt options, the output is the following: 
```
export VERILATOR_ROOT='/usr/local/share/verilator'
No stack.
[Thread debugging using libthread_db enabled]
Using host libthread_db library "/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libthread_db.so.1".
Starting Verilator 5.027 devel rev v5.026-29-ge3e7b3f2b
- Verilator.cpp:644:  Option --verilate: Start Verilation
- V3File.cpp:234:        --check-times failed: different command line
- V3ParseImp.cpp:292: parseFile: verilated_std
  Preprocessing /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_std.sv
- V3PreShell.cpp:143:     Reading /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_std.sv
- V3ParseImp.cpp:372: Lexing /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_std.sv
- V3ParseImp.cpp:292: parseFile: 4_gram_verilog_file_2215
  Preprocessing chigen_programs/10k_programs_per_k/4gram/4_gram_verilog_file_2215.v
- V3PreShell.cpp:143:     Reading chigen_programs/10k_programs_per_k/4gram/4_gram_verilog_file_2215.v
- V3ParseImp.cpp:358: Writing all preprocessed output to obj_dir/V__034_gram_verilog_file_2215__inputs.vpp
- V3ParseImp.cpp:372: Lexing chigen_programs/10k_programs_per_k/4gram/4_gram_verilog_file_2215.v
- V3LinkLevel.cpp:42: modSortByLevel()
- V3Ast.cpp:1323:     Dumping obj_dir/V__034_gram_verilog_file_2215_001_cells.tree
- V3LinkDot.cpp:3913: linkDotPrimary: 
- V3LinkJump.cpp:373: linkJump: 
- V3Ast.cpp:1323:     Dumping obj_dir/V__034_gram_verilog_file_2215_006_linkjump.tree
- V3LinkInc.cpp:306:  linkIncrements: 
- V3Ast.cpp:1323:     Dumping obj_dir/V__034_gram_verilog_file_2215_007_linkinc.tree
- V3Param.cpp:1480:   param: 
- V3LinkDot.cpp:3919: linkDotParamed: 
- V3Ast.cpp:1323:     Dumping obj_dir/V__034_gram_verilog_file_2215_009_linkdotparam.tree
- V3Dead.cpp:542:     deadifyModules: 
- V3Width.cpp:7794:   width: 
- V3Ast.cpp:1323:     Dumping obj_dir/V__034_gram_verilog_file_2215_012_width.tree
- V3WidthCommit.cpp:290:widthCommit: 
- V3Const.cpp:3978:   constifyAllLive: 
- V3Ast.cpp:1323:     Dumping obj_dir/V__034_gram_verilog_file_2215_014_const.tree
- V3Undriven.cpp:552: undrivenAll: 
- V3AssertPre.cpp:531:assertPreAll: 
- V3Ast.cpp:1323:     Dumping obj_dir/V__034_gram_verilog_file_2215_015_assertpre.tree
- V3Assert.cpp:626:   assertAll: 
- V3Ast.cpp:1323:     Dumping obj_dir/V__034_gram_verilog_file_2215_016_assert.tree
- V3LinkLevel.cpp:144:wrapTop: 
- V3Const.cpp:3945:   constifyAllLint: 
- V3Ast.cpp:1323:     Dumping obj_dir/V__034_gram_verilog_file_2215_018_const.tree
- V3SplitVar.cpp:1239:splitVariable: 
- V3Inst.cpp:618:     dearrayAll: 
- V3LinkDot.cpp:3925: linkDotArrayed: 
- V3Begin.cpp:363:    debeginAll: 
- V3Ast.cpp:1323:     Dumping obj_dir/V__034_gram_verilog_file_2215_023_begin.tree
- V3Tristate.cpp:1834:tristateAll: 

Program terminated with signal SIGKILL, Killed.
The program no longer exists.
No stack.
%Error: export VERILATOR_ROOT=/usr/local/share/verilator
%Error: ulimit -s unlimited 2>/dev/null; exec setarch --addr-no-randomize gdb /usr/local/share/verilator/bin/verilator_bin_dbg --batch --quiet --return-child-result -ex "run --cc chigen_programs/10k_programs_per_k/4gram/4_gram_verilog_file_2215.v --debug --gdbbt" -ex 'set width 0' -ex 'bt' -ex 'quit'
%Error: Command Failed ulimit -s unlimited 2>/dev/null; exec setarch --addr-no-randomize gdb /usr/local/share/verilator/bin/verilator_bin_dbg --batch --quiet --return-child-result -ex "run --cc chigen_programs/10k_programs_per_k/4gram/4_gram_verilog_file_2215.v --debug --gdbbt" -ex 'set width 0' -ex 'bt' -ex 'quit'
``` 


What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?
``` 
verilator --cc
```
What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?
```
Verilator 4.038 2020-07-11 rev v4.036-114-g0cd4a57ad
```
What OS and distribution are you using?
Ubuntu 20.04
